g.text: Scenario Trace: The Angry CFO
How anger cascades through the system over 4 turns
TURN 1
CEO proposes aggressive timeline. CTO supports.
CFO emotions: anger 0.2 -> 0.35 (disagreement). No trigger yet.
CFO stays quiet. Urgency: 54 (base). No modulation active.
State: trust=0.5, tension=0.3, dominance=0.3
TURN 2
CTO dismisses CFO concerns: 'Your numbers are conservative.'
Challenge directed at CFO -> anger 0.35 -> 0.50. Below threshold.
Urgency: 62 (base + tension). CFO bids but loses to CEO.
consecutive_events_since_bid: 3. Getting frustrated.
TURN 3
CEO reiterates timeline. CFO emotions cross threshold.
anger 0.50 -> 0.65. Triggers at 0.7. MODULATION ACTIVATED
interrupt_bias +0.4, compromise_bias -0.3, challenge_bias +0.25
Urgency: 62 + 15 = 77. CFO wins bid, interrupts.
'You're ignoring the burn-rate analysis entirely. That's reckless.'
TURN 4
CEO tries to de-escalate. CFO is still angry.
compromise_bias -0.3: CFO resists conciliatory moves
CFO doubles down: challenges CEO's growth projections aggressively.
tension > 0.8 -> escalation_risk trigger fires. System on edge.
Outcome: Coalition dynamics shift. Trust damage. escalation_risk may auto-create recovery plan.
